records.ety: [From Deep; akin to D. diepte, Icel. dpt, dpð, Goth. diupiþa.]

defs: The quality of being deep; deepness; perpendicular measurement downward from the surface, or horizontal measurement backward from the front; as, the depth of a river; the depth of a body of troops.
Profoundness; extent or degree of intensity; abundance; completeness; as, depth of knowledge, or color.
quotes: Mindful of that heavenly love Which knows no end in depth or height.

defs: That which is deep; a deep, or the deepest, part or place; the deep; the middle part; as, the depth of night, or of winter.
quotes: The depth closed me round about.

defs: The number of simple elements which an abstract conception or notion includes; the comprehension or content.
A pair of toothed wheels which work together.
The perpendicular distance from the chord to the farthest point of an arched surface.
the maximum number of times a type of procedure is reiteratively called before the last call is exited; -- of subroutines or procedures which are reentrant; -- used of call stacks.
